description: S&P Dow Jones Indices announcement of SENEA deletion from the S&P SmallCap 600
Effective date of SENEA deletion (close of business on rebalance day)
Aggregate SENEA share count held by S&P SmallCap 600 trackers (IJR, SLY, VIOO and other) as of T-2 - 1
Observed SENEA volume on T-2 vs 60-day pre-event median ADV (one-day rebalance spike test)
Pre vs post-event ADV, bid-ask spread, and Amihud illiquidity ratio
Cumulative abnormal returns from market-model and Fama-French-5 + momentum regressions across event windows
Catalog of every SENEA SEC filing and press release inside [T-1 - 30, T-2 + 30], tagged fundamental vs non-fundamental
Cumulative HML, SMB, R2000V, and packaged-foods returns over the same event windows used for IS-4
Russell 2000 / Russell 2000 Value membership and reconstitution dates inside [T-1 - 30, T-2 + 30]
Concurrent fundamentals (Seneca FY2024 reporting cycle) may explain part of the cumulative move
Mid-2023 small-cap value factor weakness can absorb part of the negative drift; HML / R2000V control required
Russell reconstitution typically lands in late June; some forced-selling volume may belong to Russell flows
S&P SmallCap 600 deletions are pre-announced; CARs over [T-1, T-2] can be near zero by the effective date even when the flow story is real
Post-deletion ADV decline can reflect general small-cap volume contraction in 2023-2024 rather than SENEA-specific liquidity loss
